name: Sausage & Chicken Jambalaya
recipeInstructions:
1. Season and brown chicken in oil (lard, bacon drippings are traditional) over medium-high heat.
2. Add sausage to the pot (cut into 1/4 inch rounds) and sauté with chicken. Remove both from the pot.
3. For a brown jambalaya, add 1-2 tablespoons brown sugar to hot oil and caramelize or make a roux using Kitchen Bouquet. For red jambalaya, skip this step (I prefer a deep chestnut-colored roux).
4. Sauté onions, celery, green pepper, and garlic until tender.
5. Return chicken and sausage to the pot. Add liquid, salt, pepper, and other desired seasonings, then bring to boil.
6. If using Kitchen Bouquet for brown jambalaya, add 1-2 tablespoons. For red jambalaya, add approximately 1/4 cup paprika; you may also use half stock and half tomato juice or V-8 for liquid.
7. Add rice and return to boil.
8. Cover and reduce heat to simmer. Cook for a total of 30 minutes.
9. After 10 minutes of cooking, remove the cover and stir the rice from top to bottom completely.
10. Add green onions and chopped tomatoes if desired.
11. For seafood jambalaya, follow steps 1-2, then return seafood to the pot.
